held: This file is NOT read by measure.mjs. score.mjs reads it to grade the blind channels.
claim: The probability the reader reserves for an unseen atom — forwardDist().reserve, and the surprise it therefore assigns to a genuine newcomer — should TRACK THE RECENT NOVELTY RATE: high right after a burst of newcomers, low after a stretch of confirmation. The live engine fails this: its reserve is novelty/(mass+novelty) with a CONSTANT novelty, so it depends only on total mass and is blind to whether newcomers have lately been arriving.

s1.gloss: office endures, then a burst of 3 new deans (newcomers at steps 5,6,7)

s5.gloss: 3 new deans first, then the office endures (newcomers at steps 0,1,2)

s3.gloss: one long tenure — the office only (newcomer at step 0 only)

s4.gloss: drone, then 3 new pitches (newcomers at steps 5,6,7)

s2.gloss: 3 new pitches first, then the drone (newcomers at steps 0,1,2)

s6.gloss: one held drone (newcomer at step 0 only)

loud_surface_is_flat.predict: reserve_const(RECENT) == reserve_const(EARLY) == reserve_const(CONFIRM) within 1e-9, in BOTH senses
loud_surface_is_flat.why: Every step deposits exactly one unit, so total gamma-mass at the probe is identical across conditions. The CONSTANT-novelty reserve is a function of that mass alone — so it is provably flat here. A method that 'wins' on this stimulus via mass is therefore caught: mass cannot tell these conditions apart.

recency_not_count.predict: RECENT and EARLY have EQUAL distinctCount (4 in sense A, 4 in sense B); only CONFIRM is lower (1).
recency_not_count.why: RECENT > EARLY under the tracked reserve cannot be a cumulative-distinct-count effect, because RECENT and EARLY carry the same count. The only thing that differs between them is WHEN the newcomers arrived — recency.

mechanism: reserve = the gamma-decayed recent newcomer RATE: noveltyAmplitude(firstSeen, now, {gamma}) = sum over prior atoms of gamma^(now-1-firstSeen), fed as `novelty` into the UNCHANGED forwardDist/surpriseAt. Context enters at the amplitude; the Born step (square-and-normalize / the KL normalization) is fixed and identical for every sense. Interior, modality-agnostic: the SAME helper and the SAME law produce the dissociation over text-proposition atoms (sense A) and tonal-pitch atoms (sense B).

verdict_rule.gap_located: reserve_const is FLAT across RECENT/EARLY/CONFIRM (fails predicted_dissociation) — the live engine cannot discriminate recent novelty from mass.
verdict_rule.capability_confirmed: reserve_tracked satisfies per_sense_order strictly in BOTH senses AND the recency_not_count control holds.
verdict_rule.interior_confirmed: capability_confirmed holds in sense A AND sense B (two different front-end maps, one law).
